title: Gina settings.json
description: Bundle runtime settings: locale, server engine, CORS response headers, file upload limits, and Socket.io config.

region.description: Locale and timezone settings for this bundle.

i18n.description: Runtime i18n configuration for the per-bundle message catalogs (bundle/locales/<culture>.json). All keys are read from the bundle's merged settings.

cookieName.description: Locale-persistence cookie name read by the per-request locale negotiation (honoured since 0.5.16). null disables cookie-based negotiation; absent or empty keeps "gina_culture".

devMissingKey.description: Dev-mode prefix marker for missing catalog keys (only applied when NODE_ENV_IS_DEV is "true"). null disables the marker.

fallbackChain.description: Optional override of the default catalog-lookup fallback chain (specific culture, then base language, then settings.region.culture, then GINA_CULTURE, then "en"). null uses the default.

cultures.description: Explicit culture allowlist for the user-signal negotiation steps (URL prefix, cookie, Accept-Language), honoured since 0.5.22: a non-empty array constrains matching to the listed cultures (staged rollouts). null or [] derives from the bundle's loaded catalogs. The bundle-default step (settings.region.culture) is never constrained.

server.description: HTTP server engine and protocol configuration.

engine.description: HTTP server engine. Defaults to "isaac" (built-in HTTP/2 server).

scheme.description: URL scheme. Use "https" for HTTP/2 (required) or "http" for HTTP/1.1 only.

allowHTTP1.description: When true, the HTTP/2 server also accepts HTTP/1.1 connections (ALPN negotiation). Defaults to true.

warmup.description: List of upstream URLs to pre-establish HTTP/2 sessions at bundle startup. Reduces latency on the first real request.

releaseWatch.description: Stale built-release watch for LOCAL production rehearsals — hard-gated on scope `local` + a non-dev env, never active on a real cluster. When enabled, the bundle watches its SOURCE tree, compares fingerprints against the manifest release stamp, surfaces staleness on GET /_gina/release/status (+ the /_gina/release/events SSE stream) and rebuilds via POST /_gina/release/rebuild with an idle-gated restart (in-flight requests + gina.registerBusyProbe application probes). Fail-closed: disabled by default.

mode.description: notify (default): surface staleness and wait for the operator. auto: rebuild on change automatically — the restart stays idle-gated.

debounceMs.description: Watch-event batch debounce window in ms. Defaults to 750.

reconcileIntervalMs.description: Optional slow re-fingerprint sweep interval in ms for event-lossy mounts; 0 (the default) disables the sweep.

restartMode.description: How a rebuild restarts the process. daemon (default): drain, then spawn `gina bundle:restart` (needs the framework daemon). supervisor: drain, then exit(0) for a container/orchestrator to respawn — the daemonless `gina-container` launch path; requires a restart-on-clean-exit policy (a k8s Deployment or `docker run --restart=always` qualifies; `--restart=on-failure` does not).

cache.description: Bundle-wide render/output-cache defaults. `type` / `store` here are folded into the runtime `server.cache` (which also carries the per-env `enable` / `path` / `ttl` / `maxEntries` from env.json), so they act as the default a route inherits when its own `cache` omits them.

type.description: Bundle-wide default cache strategy. A per-route `cache.type` overrides it; a route with `cache` set but no `type` inherits it. `memory` = in-process (volatile); `fs` = disk-backed (restart-surviving); `redis` = shared L2 across replicas + per-replica L1.

store.description: Required for `redis`: the name of a `connectors.json` entry ({ "<name>": { "connector": "redis", "host": …, "port": … } }) providing the L2 connection. Ignored for memory / fs.

integrationMode.description: How Socket.io attaches to the HTTP server.

pingTimeout.description: Time to wait for a pong before considering the connection dropped (e.g. "10s", "20s").

maxFieldsSize.description: Maximum total size of multipart form fields (e.g. "8MB", "50MB").

response.description: HTTP response header overrides, typically used for CORS.

header.description: Static response headers added to every response from this bundle.

swig.description: Opt-in override for the swig template engine. When useProject is true the bundle loads the project-pinned package from the project's node_modules instead of the framework's bundled copy — provided the project version satisfies the framework's same-major and minimum-version constraint. A rejected override falls back to the framework copy and logs a warning at bundle startup.

useProject.description: When true, attempt to load the package from <projectPath>/node_modules. When false or omitted, always load the framework's bundled copy (default, current behaviour).

package.description: npm package name to resolve. Defaults to @rhinostone/swig (native swig syntax). Set to @rhinostone/swig-twig to use the Twig-syntax frontend. The abandoned upstream "swig" name is never accepted.

template.description: Per-engine custom template loader (async loader extension point). When a loader is configured the engine resolves templates through it instead of the local filesystem, enabling remote / CDN / object-storage / in-memory template backends. Bundles without a loader render from disk exactly as before.

loader.description: Custom async loader for the swig render path. Mirrors the connectors.json convention: a named built-in `type` plus type-specific flat keys. When present, the bundle renders via the isolated per-bundle swig engine (controller.render-swig-async.js) instead of the filesystem path.

type.description: Built-in loader name. "memory" loads templates from an inline `templates` map (no network or filesystem). "http" fetches templates over HTTP(S) from a configured origin + basePath, with a TTL'd source cache and opt-in ETag revalidation.

templates.description: For type "memory": flat map of template identifier to source string.

origin.description: For type "http": scheme://host[:port] of the template origin (no path). http or https. Required for the http loader.

basePath.description: For type "http": path prefix under origin that template identifiers resolve against (root-relative, flat namespace). Defaults to "". Resolved URLs are containment-checked to stay under origin+basePath.

ttl.description: For type "http": source-cache TTL in seconds (absolute from fetch). Defaults to 60. Raise it for immutable CDN-published templates; 0 caches until evicted.

revalidate.description: For type "http": when true, a cache hit issues a conditional GET (If-None-Match) and serves cached source on 304, refreshing the TTL. Off by default (no per-hit round-trip). Turn on for live-CMS templates whose origin emits ETags.

cache.description: #TPL1 Tier-2 opt-in: reuse compiled templates across requests via the shared per-bundle engine + AsyncLocalStorage per-request context. Off by default (per-request render, no compiled-template reuse). Force-disabled in dev mode regardless so template edits are picked up live without a restart.

loader.description: Custom async loader for the nunjucks render path. Mirrors the connectors.json convention: a named built-in `type` plus type-specific flat keys. When present, the bundle renders via a per-request nunjucks Environment over this loader (controller.render-nunjucks-async.js) instead of the cached-Environment filesystem path.

type.description: Built-in loader name. "memory" loads templates from an inline `templates` map (no network or filesystem). "http" fetches templates over HTTP(S) from a configured origin + basePath, with a TTL'd source cache and opt-in ETag revalidation.

templates.description: For type "memory": flat map of template identifier to source string.

origin.description: For type "http": scheme://host[:port] of the template origin (no path). http or https. Required for the http loader.

basePath.description: For type "http": path prefix under origin that template identifiers resolve against (root-relative, flat namespace). Defaults to "". Resolved URLs are containment-checked to stay under origin+basePath.

ttl.description: For type "http": source-cache TTL in seconds (absolute from fetch). Defaults to 60. Raise it for immutable CDN-published templates; 0 caches until evicted.

revalidate.description: For type "http": when true, a cache hit issues a conditional GET (If-None-Match) and serves cached source on 304, refreshing the TTL. Off by default (no per-hit round-trip). Turn on for live-CMS templates whose origin emits ETags.

cache.description: #TPL1 Tier-2 opt-in: reuse compiled templates across requests via a shared per-request-context (AsyncLocalStorage) nunjucks Environment. Off by default (per-request Environment, no compiled-template reuse). Force-disabled in dev mode regardless so template edits are picked up live without a restart.

render.description: Template engine dispatch. Selects which delegate file (controller.render-swig.js or controller.render-nunjucks.js) handles self.render() calls for this bundle. Switching to "nunjucks" requires `npm install nunjucks` in the project root — the framework does not bundle nunjucks.

engine.description: Bundle-level template engine for self.render() HTML output. "swig" (default) uses @rhinostone/swig via controller.render-swig.js; "nunjucks" uses the project-installed nunjucks package via controller.render-nunjucks.js. When set to "nunjucks", bundle startup fails with NUNJUCKS_NOT_INSTALLED if the package is absent from the project's node_modules. An explicit template extension overrides this per render: a section whose templates.json `ext` is ".njk" (or a self.setTemplate(file, '.njk') call) renders through nunjucks, ".swig" through swig — so a single bundle can mix engines; ".html" and any other extension follow this setting.

nunjucks.description: Configuration for the nunjucks template engine (when render.engine is "nunjucks"). The package is never framework-bundled — it must exist in the project's node_modules.

package.description: npm package name to resolve in <projectPath>/node_modules. Defaults to "nunjucks" (upstream).

autoescape.description: Whether the nunjucks Environment autoescapes by default. Passed to `new nunjucks.Environment(loader, { autoescape })`.
